lesson.claim: Project Telos has a fourteenth-wave Lean replay rung package that installs/binds Lean by explicit path, compiles a bounded integer algebraic cancellation theorem file for the Navier-Stokes periodic skew-symmetry packet, refreshes formal-PDE and Lean/prover arXiv source leads, demotes those leads to SOURCE_LEAD_ONLY, updates website and official-copy surfaces, and keeps the continuous periodic integration-by-parts theorem NOT_REPLAYED and the parent Navier-Stokes problem UNVERIFIABLE.
lesson.scope: Local fourteenth-wave documentation and receipt package only. This is a Lean replay for integer algebraic cancellation lemmas, not continuous periodic integration by parts, not smooth-field formalization, not PDE correctness, not physical-fluid validation, not source-body review of arXiv papers, not exhaustive literature coverage, not global Lean PATH certification, not native BuildLang relation-invariant support, and not an accepted publication.

(scope: Local fourteenth-wave documentation and receipt package only. Judging by their refs alone (docs/outreach/FOURTEENTH-WAVE-LEAN-REPLAY-RUNG-2026-07-02.md, docs/outreach/FOURTEENTH-WAVE-CONTENT-QUEUE-2026-07-02.md, docs/research/proof-packets/navier-stokes-periodic-skew-symmetry-v0/formal/lean/PeriodicCancellationPreflight.lean, docs/outreach/receipts/fourteenth-wave/lean-periodic-cancellation-replay-2026-07-02.json, docs/outreach/receipts/fourteenth-wave/source-lead-demotion-gate.json, docs/research/official/FORMAL-REPLAY-PREFLIGHT-FOR-PDE-PACKETS-2026-07-02.md), predict what each recorded source could contribute as evidence for or against the claim.

List the checks you would run to reach your own verdict on this claim, then compare your list against what the packet's verdict implies was actually checked.
